creator: tech-lists@zyxst.net
text:
I'm not sure if the following information is of any use, but thought I'd mention it in case it is:

1. with more or less the same config, but configured to block many more addresses (so the table is many times larger i guess), but on amd64 (freebsd bhyve guest) running 13.0-p5, the command runs without error:

pf-badhost 87438 - - Using experimental "aggy" aggregator...

2182 addresses added.
2854 addresses deleted.

pf-badhost 87506 - - 
IPv4 addresses in table:  1044433099

but in dmesg there is the message that pf states limit has been reached. The vm still works/passes traffic though. The vm has 8GB vram. I've not tried increasing that yet.

here is pfctl -si

# pfctl -si
